spaceshare.overview: SpaceShare connects B2B shippers with inter-province drivers who already have a planned trip and spare capacity in their vehicle. We shipped three products — a driver mobile app, a company/admin web app, and a shared backend API — around a driver-selected delivery model: no auto-matching; drivers choose packages on their route, pack with capacity checks, then deliver with proof-of-delivery.

highlights.title: Mobile app
highlights.subtitle: Drivers · Expo + React Native
bullets: Planned routes — origin, destination, time, capacity, max deviation
Browse on-route packages and select shipments to carry
Maps, job list, delivery completion, notifications

highlights.title: Backend API
highlights.subtitle: Go · PostgreSQL · Redis
bullets: Planned routes and deviation-based package lists (PostGIS)
Status timeline, JWT auth, shared mobile + web contracts

highlights.title: Core workflow
highlights.subtitle: Driver-selected delivery
bullets: Route → on-route packages → driver picks (no auto-match)
Pack confirm → navigate → proof of delivery
List filters by route deviation today; time/capacity on roadmap

highlights.title: Product delivery
highlights.subtitle: Project management
bullets: MVP scope for inter-province B2B and pilot corridors
Backlog across API, mobile, and web
Aligned founders and engineers on roles and business rules

highlights.title: Status
highlights.subtitle: Pre-launch · pilot-ready
bullets: Core loop live on all three surfaces
Private repo — full walkthrough in demo video below

spaceshare.role: Project Manager — owned scope, specs, and delivery across the mobile app, web app, and API. Defined user flows per role (driver vs. company/admin), prioritized the backlog, and kept all surfaces aligned on the driver-selected delivery model.

my-portfolio.overview: This site is the portfolio you're browsing — a static Next.js app with App Router, Framer Motion, and Three.js. I use it to present projects, skills, experience, and a downloadable resume, with smooth section animations and a dark zinc theme with red accents. It deploys to GitHub Pages with an optional `/portfolio` base path.
highlights: App Router layout with reusable sections for hero, projects, skills, experience, education, and contact
Framer Motion for scroll-linked and hover animations across cards and navigation
Three.js / React Three Fiber for immersive 3D visuals in the hero
JSON-driven content in `data/` for projects and skills — easy to extend without touching layout code
Static export (`output: 'export'`) for GitHub Pages, with `getImagePath()` handling basePath-aware assets
Resume download from `public/resume/` and responsive layout from mobile through desktop
my-portfolio.role: Sole developer — designed the information architecture, built all UI components, wired data files, configured static export and GitHub Pages deployment.

figure-shop.overview: Figure Shop is a full-stack marketplace for buying and selling collectible figures. I split the system into a Node.js/Express REST API backed by MongoDB and an EJS storefront/admin dashboard that consumes those APIs through Axios — covering catalog CRUD, image uploads, discounts, carts, orders, payments, and analytics.
highlights: Session-based authentication with bcrypt hashing and username availability checks
Product catalog with category tagging, Multer image uploads (stored as base64 in MongoDB), and discount scheduling
Shopping cart, order, payment method/type, and address endpoints for the full checkout pipeline
Analytics routes aggregating best-selling categories and per-user spending
Admin EJS views for product/discount CRUD plus customer shop, product detail, cart, and order tracking
Docker Compose option to run MariaDB seed, backend, and frontend together without local Node/MySQL setup
figure-shop.role: Full-stack developer — architected API contracts, implemented backend controllers/routers and frontend Axios integration, and documented deployment with Docker Compose.

asal.overview: Another Story Another Legacy (ASAL) is an online TTRPG platform built for the "new normal" — when in-person tabletop sessions were hard to arrange. Our team recreated the D&D-style experience in Unity so players can create characters, roll dice, fight, chat, and adventure together in real time, with Firebase handling auth and live game state.
highlights: Character creation with customizable class, race, and abilities
Virtual dice rolling and a combat system for strategic battles
Story design tools for Dungeon Masters to craft custom maps and scenarios
Email/password sign-in and registration via Firebase Authentication
Realtime Database persistence for characters, inventory, and custom maps
Multiplayer, in-game chat, and game log for collaborative sessions
Shipped as a Windows PC build (.exe) for an optimized desktop experience
asal.role: Backend-focused contributor (Jarukit Pan-Iam, 65070501006) — designed Firebase Realtime Database schemas for characters, inventories, and maps; built C# services syncing gameplay data and secure account flows so DMs and players could collaborate live.

wifi-authentication-attack.overview: This cybersecurity mini project simulates real-world Wi-Fi attack workflows in a controlled lab — WPA/WPA2 handshake capture, deauthentication, Hashcat cracking, and an Evil Twin captive portal — packaged behind an interactive Bash menu on Kali Linux (VMware) with a USB monitor-mode adapter.
highlights: Interactive `wifi_attack.sh` menu for adapter setup, monitor mode, scanning, and attack workflows
Handshake capture locked to a target AP and channel, with `.cap` → `.22000` conversion for Hashcat
Deauthentication via `aireplay-ng` (targeted) or `mdk4` (broadcast) to force client reconnects
Cracking pipelines: Rockyou, custom wordlists, rule-based (`best64.rule`), and mask patterns
Evil Twin stack: `hostapd` fake AP, `dnsmasq` DHCP/DNS redirect, Flask captive portal on port 80
Credential logging to `credentials.txt` for lab analysis and hardening discussion
wifi-authentication-attack.role: Sole developer — implemented Bash orchestration, Flask captive portal templates, and documented the virtual lab setup (Kali on VMware, TP-Link Archer T2U Plus in monitor mode).
wifi-authentication-attack.disclaimer: For educational and research purposes only. Do not use on networks you do not own or lack explicit permission to test. You are solely responsible for any misuse.

pacman-fpga.overview: Pacman FPGA is a complete Pac-Man–style game implemented in Verilog for Xilinx Artix-7 boards (tested on Digilent Basys 3). The design drives 640×480@60 Hz VGA output, animates Pac-Man and two ghosts, manages collectible scoring and heart-based lives, and includes a Vivado project ready for bitstream generation.
highlights: 640×480 VGA pipeline with background ROM, walls, layered sprites, and compositor priority
Pac-Man motion FSM with wall avoidance, direction-dependent sprites, and post-hit ghost animation
Two autonomous ghosts with independent speed offsets and collision detection against Pac-Man
Collectible stars respawning across eight maze regions with a 4-digit score counter
Game state machine: start → play → hit (invincibility window) → game over → restart via `start_btn`
Optional seven-segment score display; shipped bitstream from Vivado 2015.4 included in the repo
pacman-fpga.role: Hardware developer — designed top-level integration, sprite ROMs, FSMs (`Pacman_control`, ghosts, stars, hearts), and maze collision logic; validated on Basys 3 with push-button controls.

moded.overview: ModEd is the capstone full-stack platform for CPE494 at KMUTT — a large collaborative codebase where 30+ students shipped modules on a shared Go backend, JavaScript UI, PostgreSQL, and VS Code Dev Container workflow. I owned the HR module: employee data model, leave-approval flows, and integration hooks into the rest of the system.
highlights: HR module ownership: employee entities, leave requests, and approval workflows wired to shared APIs
Developed inside the team Dev Container (Docker, Go, Postgres pre-installed) for consistent environments
Followed team git workflow: commit → `git pull --rebase` → resolve conflicts → push
Integrated with the broader ModEd architecture referenced from the OOAD2568 course resources
Collaborated at scale (~30+ contributors) on a monorepo-style fullstack codebase
moded.role: HR module lead — designed the employee/leave domain model, implemented backend and UI integration points, and kept the module aligned with shared Dev Container and API conventions.
